speaker: HUME
text: Come, my masters. The Duchess, I tell you,

speaker: HUME
text: expects performance of your promises.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Master Hume, we are therefore provided.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Will her Ladyship behold and hear our

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: exorcisms?

speaker: HUME
text: Ay, what else? Fear you not her courage.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I have heard her reported to be a

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: woman of an invincible spirit. But it shall be convenient,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Master Hume, that you be by her aloft

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: while we be busy below; and so, I pray you, go, in

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: God’s name, and leave us.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Mother Jourdain, be you prostrate and grovel on

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text:
the earth.
                 
                She
                 
                lies
                 
                face
                 
                downward
                .
               John Southwell,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: read you; and let us to our work.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Well said, my masters, and welcome all. To

speaker: DUCHESS
text: this gear, the sooner the better.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Patience, good lady. Wizards know their times.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Deep night, dark night, the silent of the night,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The time of night when Troy was set on fire,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The time when screech owls cry and bandogs howl,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And spirits walk, and ghosts break up their graves—

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That time best fits the work we have in hand.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Madam, sit you, and fear not. Whom we raise

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: We will make fast within a hallowed verge.

speaker: SPIRIT
text: Adsum.

speaker: JOURDAIN
text: Asmath,

speaker: JOURDAIN
text: By the eternal God, whose name and power

speaker: JOURDAIN
text: Thou tremblest at, answer that I shall ask,

speaker: JOURDAIN
text: For till thou speak, thou shalt not pass from hence.

speaker: SPIRIT
text: Ask what thou wilt. That I had said and done!

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: First of the King: What shall of him become?

speaker: SPIRIT
text: The duke yet lives that Henry shall depose,

speaker: SPIRIT
text: But him outlive and die a violent death.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: What fates await the Duke of Suffolk?

speaker: SPIRIT
text: By water shall he die and take his end.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: What shall befall the Duke of Somerset?

speaker: SPIRIT
text: Let him shun castles.

speaker: SPIRIT
text: Safer shall he be upon the sandy plains

speaker: SPIRIT
text: Than where castles mounted stand.

speaker: SPIRIT
text: Have done, for more I hardly can endure.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Descend to darkness and the burning lake!

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: False fiend, avoid!

speaker: YORK
text: Lay hands upon these traitors and their trash.

speaker: YORK
text:
To
                 
                Jourdain
                .
                 
              Beldam, I think we watched you at an inch.

speaker: YORK
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                Duchess
                ,
                 
                aloft
                .
                 
              What, madam, are you there? The King and commonweal

speaker: YORK
text: Are deeply indebted for this piece of pains.

speaker: YORK
text: My Lord Protector will, I doubt it not,

speaker: YORK
text: See you well guerdoned for these good deserts.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Not half so bad as thine to England’s king,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Injurious duke, that threatest where’s no cause.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: True, madam, none at all. What call you this?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Away with them! Let them be clapped up close

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And kept asunder.—You, madam, shall with us.—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Stafford, take her to thee.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: We’ll see your trinkets here all forthcoming.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: All away!

speaker: YORK
text: Lord Buckingham, methinks you watched her well.

speaker: YORK
text: A pretty plot, well chosen to build upon!

speaker: YORK
text: Now, pray, my lord, let’s see the devil’s writ.

speaker: YORK
text: What have we here?

speaker: YORK
text: The duke yet lives that Henry shall depose,

speaker: YORK
text: But him outlive and die a violent death.

speaker: YORK
text: Why, this is just Aio te, Aeacida,

speaker: YORK
text: Romanos vincere posse. Well, to the rest:

speaker: YORK
text: Tell me what fate awaits the Duke of Suffolk?

speaker: YORK
text: By water shall he die and take his end.

speaker: YORK
text: What shall betide the Duke of Somerset?

speaker: YORK
text: Let him shun castles;

speaker: YORK
text: Safer shall he be upon the sandy plains

speaker: YORK
text: Than where castles mounted stand.

speaker: YORK
text: Come, come, my lord, these oracles

speaker: YORK
text: Are hardly attained and hardly understood.

speaker: YORK
text: The King is now in progress towards Saint Albans;

speaker: YORK
text: With him the husband of this lovely lady.

speaker: YORK
text: Thither goes these news as fast as horse can carry them—

speaker: YORK
text: A sorry breakfast for my Lord Protector.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your Grace shall give me leave, my lord of York,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To be the post, in hope of his reward.

speaker: YORK
text: At your pleasure, my good lord.

speaker: YORK
text: Who’s within there, ho!

speaker: YORK
text: Invite my lords of Salisbury and Warwick

speaker: YORK
text: To sup with me tomorrow night. Away!
